name: agent-loop-designer
description: Turn `$agent-loop-designer <thing I keep doing>` into the smallest repeatable Codex loop, with visible human-steerable threads, private subagents, WSL-aware worktree fan-out, and optional Grit coordination.

interface.shortDescription: Make recurring Codex work loop-first from one simple command.
interface.longDescription: Agent Loop Designer lets you type `$agent-loop-designer <describe the thing I keep doing>` and turns that recurring work into the smallest executable loop that fits the task. It loopifies by default whenever a task has repeated review, repair, follow-up, monitoring, handoff, or decision points, while keeping explicit stop conditions, loop budgets, and human gates. The main agent stays as a thin coordinator. Exploration that the user may read, interrupt, steer, or use for decisions runs in visible threads; private background research, triage, review, and summarization can go to subagents that return compact packets. Independent candidates and code-editing work use separate worktree-backed threads; in WSL, it prefers manual Linux-native git worktrees under the saved project checkout instead of Codex-managed worktrees on Windows storage. When candidate ownership is symbol-addressable, loops can use Grit for local symbol locks, `.grit/worktrees/<agent-id>/` worker checkouts, coordinator thread context gathering, and guarded `grit done` integration. It includes `$architecture-cascade` for running improve-codebase-architecture discovery in a visible architecture explorer thread, creating candidate worktree-backed threads, and acting on safe candidates automatically in isolated worktrees. Durable loops include a failure-learning rule so repeat failures caused by this plugin feed back into skill or reference updates.
